title: Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium leader working toward hospital ownership
summary: Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon With a new leader at the helm, the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium is working toward a long-held goal: ownership of the Anchorage hospital that it already manages. The Alaska Native Medical Center, the state’s largest Native hospital, stands out as the only facility in the tribal consortium’s sprawling Midtown campus that is owned by the Indian Health Service. That split between ownership and management has turned out to be burdensome, said Natasha Singh, the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium’s newly appointed president and c...

title: AST DV Assault: Kwethluk [AK25027587]
summary: On March 25, 2025, the Alaska State Troopers in Bethel received a report of an assault in Kwethluk. Troopers responded to the village and investigation revealed that Moses Jacob, 26, of Kwethluk had assaulted a domestic partner by punching them in the face and causing injury. Troopers located Jacob and placed him under arrest for Assault 3rd Degree involving domestic violence as he had been convicted on 3 occasions of Assault within the last 10 years. Jacob was remanded to the Yukon Kuskokwim Correctional Center without incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Fail to Yield: Seward [AK25027493]
summary: On 03/25/2025, Seward AST initiated a traffic stop at approximately 71 Mile of the Seward Highway on a blue Honda CRV that was traveling 84 MPH in a 65 MPH zone. When turning around on the vehicle, the vehicle accelerated and failed to yield. Thusly a pursuit was initiated. The vehicle drove upwards of 100 MPH during the pursuit, drove erratically, and went into the opposing lane of travel multiple times. The driver nearly went head on with a semi-truck driver. While in pursuit, AST learned that the vehicle was just recently stolen out of the Girdwood area. A tire deflation device was used at approximately 45 mile of the Seward Highway, which was set by another member of Seward AST. The vehicle continued attempting to elude but was ultimately blocked/pinned in by three members of Seward AS
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: approximately 71 Mile of the Seward Highway on a blue Honda CRV that was traveling 84 MPH in a 65 MPH zone

title: AST Motor Vehicle Collision - Serious Injury: Wasilla [AK25027494]
summary: On 03/25/2025 at approximately 1525 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to mile 8 Knik Goose Bay Road in Wasilla for a motor vehicle collision involving the ejection of an occupant. Serious injuries were reported. The roadway was shut down intermittently for scene investigation. No alcohol or other impairment is suspected. Investigation is ongoing.

title: AST Stolen Vehicle Recovery, Theft: North Pole [AK25027299]

title: Are Arctic Viruses “Zombies”?

title: Alaska Sockeye Season 2025: Here’s Why It’s the Year to Limit Out Fast
summary: If you’ve ever dreamed of standing knee-deep in icy turquoise Kenai River water with sockeye salmon charging upstream around you — 2025 is your year. With strong run forecasts , flexible regulations , and ideal early-July timing , this summer is shaping up to be one of the best sockeye seasons in recent memory . At Kenai Fly Fish ,...
